cold.title: Rake out winter thatch
cold.description: Remove dead grass buildup that blocks water and nutrients from reaching the soil. Rake vigorously in both directions before the first mow.

cold.title: Apply slow-release fertilizer
cold.description: Use a balanced 10-10-10 fertilizer once soil temps reach 55 degrees. Applying too early wastes nutrients before cool-season grass is actively growing.

cold.title: Repair bare patches
cold.description: Loosen bare areas with a rake, apply grass seed matched to your lawn type, and cover lightly with straw to retain moisture until germination.

moderate.title: Apply pre-emergent herbicide
moderate.description: Prevent summer annual weeds like crabgrass and goosegrass by applying pre-emergent before soil temps reach 55 degrees. Timing is everything.

moderate.title: Aerate compacted areas
moderate.description: Use a core aerator on high-traffic zones to improve water penetration and root depth. Follow with compost topdressing for the best results.

moderate.title: Begin weekly mowing at 3 to 4 inches
moderate.description: Set your mower blade high this time of year. Taller grass shades out weed seedlings and retains moisture as temperatures begin to climb.

warm.title: Begin weekly mowing schedule
warm.description: Warm-season grasses — Bermuda, Zoysia, and St. Augustine — are actively growing now. Mow weekly at the recommended height for your grass type.

warm.title: Apply nitrogen-rich fertilizer
warm.description: Warm-season grasses respond strongly to nitrogen in spring. Use a slow-release fertilizer with an N-P-K ratio like 15-0-15 for deep root development.

warm.title: Check and calibrate irrigation
warm.description: Inspect all sprinkler heads for blockages or misalignment. Aim for 1 inch of water per week across all zones, adjusted for local rainfall.

tropical.title: Monitor for chinch bugs
tropical.description: Chinch bugs thrive as temperatures rise. Look for yellowing patches that do not respond to watering — part the grass and inspect near the soil surface.

tropical.title: Fertilize with high-nitrogen formula
tropical.description: Tropical grasses are heavy feeders. Apply a slow-release nitrogen fertilizer at the start of the rainy season to fuel rapid, healthy growth.

tropical.title: Trim edges and overgrowth
tropical.description: Tropical growth rates accelerate sharply in April. Edge sidewalks and beds weekly and trim back any grass encroaching into garden beds.
